section_header: GHG REDUCTION TARGETS / ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
policy_statement: City of Chicago aims to reduce city GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 (using 2017 inventory levels as baseline).
verbatim_text:
The 2022 CAP aims to chart an equitable path to reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040.

Guided by the strategies and actions of this plan, the City of Chicago aims to reduce the city's level of GHG emissions by 62% by 2040, based on 2017 GHG inventory levels.
extraction_rationale: Citywide quantified emissions reduction target with deadline explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide GHG reduction target creates an overarching pathway that drives policies and actions to reduce emissions across sectors.

extraction_rationale: Funded program with a quantified deliverable and timeframe.
mechanism_description: Planting and maintaining more urban trees increases canopy cover, which reduces urban heat and sequesters carbon, lowering climate impacts.

policy_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: Guided by the 2013 Climate Action Plan and a review of recent emissions trends and climate actions, we have identified a suite of near-term priority actions, which will help the City remain on track to meet our climate protection goals. The actions are presented below and summarized in Table 1.
extraction_rationale: Introduces a set of near-term priority actions followed by distinct action items under the TRANSPORTATION heading.
mechanism_description: The city prioritizes near-term transportation-related climate actions intended to reduce transportation greenhouse gas emissions, but the specific mechanism is not described in the provided text.

section_header: Improving mobility through pricing
policy_statement: Develop and release a strategy to address congestion and transportation emissions through pricing, coupled with investments in expanded transit and electrification in underserved communities.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.

extraction_rationale: Specific planned intervention (pricing strategy) with linked investment focus; presented as an action item under the TRANSPORTATION near-term priorities.
mechanism_description: Implement congestion pricing and reinvest revenues in transit and electrification to reduce car travel and shift trips to cleaner modes, lowering transportation emissions.

section_header: Electric vehicle readiness ordinance for new construction
policy_statement: Pass a new electric vehicle readiness ordinance in 2018 to ensure new construction or renovation of parking structures is built with electric vehicle infrastructure.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: Pass a new electric vehicle readiness ordinance in 2018, which will ensure new construction or renovation of parking structures is built with electric vehicle infrastructure.
extraction_rationale: Clear commitment with a deadline (2018) to pass an ordinance.
mechanism_description: Mandating EV-ready infrastructure in new or renovated parking structures expands charging availability, enabling greater electric vehicle adoption and associated emissions reductions.

section_header: Charging station network map & strategy

extraction_rationale: Time-bound deliverable (2018 map) and specified intended use (guiding investment/equity).
mechanism_description: Publishing and using an optimal siting map guides investment to expand EV charging infrastructure, which reduces adoption barriers and enables increased electric vehicle uptake.

section_header: Ride share and taxi fleet electrification
policy_statement: Work with stakeholders to create recommendations for electrifying shared/for-hire fleets, determine a year after which all such vehicles must be electric, and prepare to permit only electric vehicles for for-hire use.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: The city will work with these stakeholders to create recommendations on how to electrify shared vehicle fleets, determine a year after which all such vehicles must be electric, and prepare for the City to begin permitting only electric vehicles for for-hire use.
extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory preparation (permitting only EVs) and defined planning outputs; listed as a near-term action.
mechanism_description: Requiring shared/for-hire fleets to switch to electric vehicles reduces fossil fuel combustion from vehicles and lowers transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Green Fleet Action Plan update
policy_statement: Create a new Green Fleet Action Plan by the end of 2018 to accelerate electrification of the municipal fleet and phase out fossil fuel use in municipal vehicles.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.

extraction_rationale: Clear time-bound commitment (end of 2018) to create a new plan with defined objectives.
mechanism_description: A green fleet action plan accelerates electrification of municipal vehicles, displacing fossil fuel use and reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: GHG EMISSIONS ASSESSMENT
policy_statement: Near-term climate action priorities to assess GHG emissions impacts of City decisions.
verbatim_text: The City of Seattle does not current have a city-wide policy for tracking the carbon impacts of policies or projects, which limits our ability to fully understand the cost or savings related to carbon emissions.
extraction_rationale: This heading introduces a discrete action item immediately below; treated as a parent for the listed action.
mechanism_description: Implementing a city-wide carbon impact tracking system improves emissions accounting and decision support so future policies and projects can be evaluated and guided toward lower-GHG outcomes.

section_header: Assess GHG Emissions Impact of City decisions
policy_statement: Issue an Executive Order directing City departments to assess the GHG emissions impact of City plans, policies, and major investments, with OSE to define scope and method by October 2018.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities to assess GHG emissions impacts of City decisions.
verbatim_text: Issue an Executive Order directing City departments to assess the GHG emissions impact of City plans, policies, and major investments. The Mayor will direct the Office of Sustainability & Environment to work with departments to define the types of projects to assess, the assessment method, and how to account for the broader costs of carbon, by October 2018.
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (Executive Order) plus a dated implementation milestone (October 2018).
mechanism_description: Require GHG impact assessments for city plans and major investments to inform decision-making and steer projects toward lower-emission options.

section_header: BUILDINGS

section_header: Establish new 2030 Challenge pilot for 20 upgraded, high performing projects by 2025

parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.

section_header: City Light Whole Building Pay for Performance (P4P) Programs

section_header: Improve municipal building energy efficiency and reduce carbon emissions by 2025

parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.

extraction_rationale: Defined action with a quantified scale (18,000 homes) and specified program elements (financing/incentives).

## APPROACH 4:
## REDUCE TRANSPORTATION-RELATED FUEL CONSUMPTION
extraction_rationale: Named approach with multiple targets/actions listed beneath.
mechanism_description: Reduce transportation fuel consumption by shifting trips to more efficient or low/zero-emission travel options, leading to lower transport-related greenhouse gas emissions.

extraction_rationale: Quantified mode-shift target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Reduce reliance on single-occupant vehicle trips by shifting travel to lower-carbon modes, thereby cutting transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

extraction_rationale: Quantified electrification targets for County fleet with deadline.
mechanism_description: Electrifying government fleet vehicles reduces fossil fuel use and tailpipe emissions, lowering overall transport-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

extraction_rationale: Quantified community vehicle electrification target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Increase adoption of electric vehicles in the community to replace internal combustion vehicles, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 4: REDUCE TRANSPORTATION-RELATED FUEL CONSUMPTION

section_header: Actions to reduce vehicle and mobile equipment usage:

extraction_rationale: Specific policy/program design commitment aimed at reducing transport emissions through telecommuting.
mechanism_description: Expanding telecommuting reduces commuting travel demand, lowering vehicle fuel consumption and transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Actions to expand effective low-carbon mobility options:

extraction_rationale: Concrete analytical action supporting fleet electrification decisions.
mechanism_description: Conducting an electrification analysis supports replacing fossil-fuel fleet vehicles with battery-electric vehicles, lowering transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Actions to Reduce Emissions at PortMiami, Airports & Other Commercial Hubs:

extraction_rationale: Specific technology deployment action to cut emissions from aircraft operations.
mechanism_description: Deploying visual docking guidance at airport gates reduces aircraft ground-idling time, cutting fuel use and emissions from airport operations.

section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
policy_statement: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
verbatim_text:
## APPROACH 5:
## EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
extraction_rationale: Named approach with multiple targets/actions under it.
mechanism_description: Expanding and safeguarding green and blue spaces conserves ecosystems and their carbon storage while providing resilience benefits such as reduced urban heat and improved flood/stormwater buffering.

section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
policy_statement: Increase community-wide tree canopy to 30% coverage by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
verbatim_text: -   Expand community-wide tree canopy to 30% coverage by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified canopy target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Expanding urban tree canopy increases vegetation cover, which sequesters carbon and reduces local heat through shading and evapotranspiration.

section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
policy_statement: Ensure all County facilities within the Urban Development Boundary attain at least 30% average canopy coverage and facilities outside the UDB attain at least 50% average canopy coverage by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
verbatim_text: -   Ensure that all County facilities within the UDB (per 2013 delineation) shall attain an average of  at  least  30%  canopy  coverage  and  all  County  facilities  outside  the  Urban  Development Boundary (UDB) shall attain an average of at least 50% canopy coverage by 2030
extraction_rationale: Binding 'shall' language with quantified canopy coverage targets and deadline.
mechanism_description: Increase tree canopy coverage on government facilities to provide cooling/heat mitigation and additional carbon sequestration.

section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
policy_statement: Double the total non-wetland acreage of natural habitat in preservation.
parent_statement: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
verbatim_text: -   Double the total non-wetland acreage of natural habitat in preservation
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified conservation action (doubling preserved non-wetland habitat).
mechanism_description: Protecting additional natural habitat from development preserves ecosystems and stored carbon by preventing land conversion and degradation.

section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
policy_statement: Develop a mitigation policy, based on area impacted, to ensure County-approved development results in a net increase to green infrastructure by the development's completion date.
parent_statement: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
verbatim_text: -   Develop  a  mitigation  policy,  based  on  area  impacted,  to  ensure  that  County-approved development results in a net increase to green infrastructure by the development's completion date
extraction_rationale: Specific policy development commitment with defined intended outcome (net increase in green infrastructure).
mechanism_description: Requiring a net increase in green infrastructure for new development increases permeable/vegetated area, improving stormwater management and reducing urban heat impacts.

section_header: APPROACH 6: CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY
policy_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy.
verbatim_text:
## APPROACH 6:
## CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY
extraction_rationale: Named approach with multiple specific targets and actions listed beneath.
mechanism_description: Diverting non-recycled waste to waste-to-energy facilities reduces landfill methane while generating energy that can displace more carbon-intensive power sources.

section_header: CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY APPROACH 6:
policy_statement: Generate 48 GWh/year of electricity from cogeneration at wastewater plants by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy.
verbatim_text: - 48 GWh/year of electricity from cogeneration at wastewater plants by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified energy generation target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Install cogeneration at wastewater plants to use captured biogas for electricity, reducing methane emissions and offsetting fossil electricity generation.

section_header: CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY APPROACH 6:
policy_statement: Convert 50% of non-recycled garbage to energy by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy.
verbatim_text: - 50% of non-recycled garbage converted to energy by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified conversion target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Convert non-recycled solid waste into energy instead of landfilling, reducing methane emissions and generating energy that can offset fossil fuel use.

section_header: CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY APPROACH 6:
policy_statement: Increase recycling rates by 10% and cut non-recyclables contamination in half.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy.
verbatim_text: - 10% increase in recycling rates and cut non-recyclables 'contamination' in half
extraction_rationale: Quantified recycling improvement and contamination reduction targets.
mechanism_description: Improving recycling performance and reducing contamination increases diversion of waste from landfill, thereby avoiding methane emissions from landfilled waste.

section_header: CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY APPROACH 6:
policy_statement: Build a new, more efficient electricity-from-waste facility with onsite solar, designed to charge electric vehicles in the County fleet.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy.
verbatim_text: -   Build a new, more efficient electricity-from-waste facility with onsite solar, designed to charge electric vehicles in the County fleet
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure investment action described under Approach 6.
mechanism_description: Convert non-recycled waste into electricity (with added onsite solar) to recover energy and avoid landfill methane, displacing more emissions-intensive energy used for operations such as fleet EV charging.

section_header: CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY APPROACH 6:
policy_statement: Upgrade cogeneration at wastewater treatment plants to produce 9.6 megawatts of electricity from biogas.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy.
verbatim_text: -   Upgrade cogeneration at wastewater treatment plants to produce 9.6 megawatts of electricity from biogas
extraction_rationale: Quantified capacity action (9.6 MW) to be achieved via upgrades.
mechanism_description: Upgrade wastewater-plant cogeneration to use biogas for on-site electricity generation, capturing methane and displacing fossil electricity generation.

section_header: CONVERT WASTE TO ENERGY APPROACH 6:
policy_statement: Upgrade landfill gas collection at the South Dade Landfill.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy.
verbatim_text: -   Upgrade landfill gas collection at the South Dade Landfill
extraction_rationale: Concrete operational/infrastructure upgrade commitment.
mechanism_description: Upgrading landfill gas collection increases methane capture from decomposing waste, reducing fugitive landfill methane emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text:
## APPROACH 7:
## REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
extraction_rationale: Named approach with quantified targets and multiple actions listed beneath.
mechanism_description: Reducing waste and water use lowers landfill-related emissions and reduces water-demand-related energy and resource consumption.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Reduce landfill waste per person 50% by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: - Reduce landfill waste per person 50% by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified per-capita landfill waste reduction target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Reducing the amount of waste disposed in landfills lowers methane generation from anaerobic decomposition, reducing GHG emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Reduce water consumption per person 30% by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: - Reduce water consumption per person 30% by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified per-capita water reduction target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Adopt water efficiency standards and programs to reduce per-person water consumption.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Create a community-wide food rescue plan in collaboration with community-based organizations, businesses, and farmers.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: -   Create a community-wide food rescue plan in collaboration with community-based organizations, businesses, and farmers
extraction_rationale: Specific plan commitment to reduce food waste (a methane/emissions source).
mechanism_description: Implementing a food rescue program diverts edible food from disposal, reducing landfill-bound organic waste and avoiding methane emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Complete a Countywide solid waste characterization study and complete regular updates.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: -   Complete a Countywide solid waste characterization study and complete regular updates
extraction_rationale: Concrete data/assessment action with ongoing updates to support waste reduction policy.
mechanism_description: Conducting and updating waste characterization studies improves waste-stream data, which supports planning and targeting of diversion strategies that can reduce landfill-related emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Require waste reduction plans for all large events.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: -   Require waste reduction plans for all large events
extraction_rationale: Regulatory requirement ('Require') applying to large events.
mechanism_description: Mandating waste reduction planning for large events reduces and diverts waste from landfills, which lowers methane emissions from landfill decomposition.

section_header: LETTER FROM MAYOR DANIELLA LEVINE CAVA
policy_statement: Implement the Climate Action Strategy (CAS) to cut Miami-Dade communitywide greenhouse gas emissions 50% by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
verbatim_text: Officially launched on Oct. 27, 2021, this plan describes work taking place on multiple fronts to cut MiamiDade's greenhouse gas emissions 50 percent by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified emissions-reduction targets with deadlines (50% by 2030; net zero by 2050) tied to an implemented strategy.
mechanism_description: Implementing a communitywide climate action strategy sets an economy-wide emissions target that guides and coordinates policies and investments to reduce greenhouse gas emissions over time.

section_header: NEW 2030 CLIMATE EQUITY GOALS FOR AUSTIN — Sustainable Buildings
policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Sustainable Buildings

- Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified 2030 building-sector targets; followed by multiple numbered strategies in the Sustainable Buildings section.
mechanism_description: Set stringent emissions/energy performance requirements for new and existing buildings to reduce onsite fossil fuel use and overall building energy-related emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Ensure benefits of building decarbonization programs flow to low-income communities and communities of color through comprehensive energy poverty mitigation and partnerships.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Ensure benefits flow to low-income communities and communities of color

Pursue a comprehensive energy poverty mitigation strategy by partnering with trusted community organizations, affordable housing developers, and schools in equitable outreach and program development.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (energy poverty mitigation strategy and partnerships) tied to the buildings goal.
mechanism_description: Partnering with trusted community organizations and institutions improves equitable outreach and program design, increasing low-income and marginalized communities’ access to building decarbonization and energy poverty mitigation benefits.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy consumption via outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanded benchmarking for existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Enhance understanding of energy consumption

Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy savings opportunities, benefits, and climate impacts of energy consumption. This will be done through direct outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanding benchmarking requirements for all existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
extraction_rationale: Concrete programmatic actions (benchmarking expansion; data access; outreach).
mechanism_description: Expand benchmarking and data transparency so residents and building owners better understand energy use, creating a market/behavioral signal that motivates energy efficiency improvements and reduces emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon building milestones through new building energy codes/amendments and incentives/education for existing building decarbonization, in collaboration with utilities and with equity focus.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Achieve energy-efficient, net-zero carbon buildings

Achieve goal milestones for net-zero carbon buildings through new building energy codes, amendments, and other methods. Engage owners and operators of existing buildings to decarbonize through incentives and education for contractors and occupants. Collaborate with local utilities to implement equitable emission reduction strategies.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy mechanisms (codes, amendments, incentives, education, utility collaboration).
mechanism_description: Update and enforce new-building energy codes (with supporting programs for existing buildings) to reduce building energy consumption and associated emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging building-related technologies by partnering with local unions and education/advocacy organizations serving low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Ensure equitable workforce development for emerging technologies

Prioritize investment in local emissions reduction and create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging technologies by partnering with local unions, education, and advocacy organizations that serve low-income communities and communities of color.
extraction_rationale: Defines a workforce development intervention with specified partners and equity focus.
mechanism_description: Training and workforce partnerships expand skilled labor capacity, enabling faster and broader deployment of building-related emissions-reduction technologies.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Refrigerants
policy_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions-reduction target with a deadline; followed by a list of strategies.
mechanism_description: Implementing refrigerant management practices reduces refrigerant leaks and releases, thereby lowering fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting and high-GWP refrigerants to increase capture and safe destruction.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Capture and destroy old refrigerants

Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting substances and global warming potential (GWP) refrigerants.
extraction_rationale: Specific program intervention (destruction program with pricing mechanism).
mechanism_description: A pricing-backed refrigerant destruction program increases recovery and safe destruction of high-GWP refrigerants, reducing fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Move forward with building code amendments and other local actions to allow/encourage low and no-GWP refrigerants as soon as feasible.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Improve building codes to encourage cleaner refrigerants

Closely follow developments in revised building codes that allow the use of low and no GWP refrigerants... and move forward with code amendments and other local action as soon as feasible.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/code change commitment related to refrigerants.
mechanism_description: Update building codes to enable and promote low/no-GWP refrigerants, reducing fugitive greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Create incentives for refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair in partnership with food retail, restaurants, warehouses, and HVAC companies, prioritizing outreach to small businesses owned by people of color.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Create incentives for leak detection and repair

Partner with grocery stores, convenience stores, restaurants... to create an incentive for designing and tracking refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair.
extraction_rationale: Concrete incentive-based policy with named partner sectors.
mechanism_description: Incentivizing leak detection, prevention, and repair in refrigeration/HVAC systems reduces refrigerant leakage, cutting fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Create an awareness, education, and training campaign for HVAC service providers and related stakeholders; lead by example with refrigerant leak detection/repair at City-owned facilities.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Awareness and training for HVAC service providers

Create an awareness, education, and training campaign... Lead by example by implementing a refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair program at City-owned facilities.
extraction_rationale: Specific campaign plus a City operational commitment (lead by example).
mechanism_description: Training and leak detection/repair programs reduce refrigerant leakage from HVAC equipment, lowering fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Reduce the volume/use of refrigerants, particularly high-GWP refrigerants, by expanding and prioritizing codes and incentives favoring passive design, reduction, and efficiency.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Reduce the volume of refrigerants

Emphasize the link between design and refrigerant use by reducing and preventing the use of refrigerants... particularly those with high GWP.

How we'll get there:

- Ensure codes and incentives that favor passive design, reduction, and efficiency are expanded and prioritized.
extraction_rationale: Concrete policy direction to use codes/incentives to reduce refrigerant use.
mechanism_description: Expand codes and incentives for passive design and efficiency to reduce reliance on high-GWP refrigerants, lowering refrigerant-related fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Embodied Carbon
policy_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline*.

*The embodied carbon associated with the construction and materials must not exceed 500 kg CO2e/m2 (~100 lbs. CO2e/sf) per project.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target plus specific performance threshold; followed by multiple strategies.
mechanism_description: Require lower-embodied-carbon construction materials, thereby reducing lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions associated with building construction.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to deliver healthy, low-carbon buildings, prioritizing community facilities serving low-income communities and communities of color first.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Lead by example through design and construction standards

In partnership with other cities and states, develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to result in healthy, low-carbon buildings.
extraction_rationale: Specific government procurement/standards action (lead by example).
mechanism_description: Adopting low-carbon design, construction, and procurement standards results in buildings that use less energy and emit fewer greenhouse gases during operation.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs (e.g., expedited permitting and Austin Energy Green Building) and transition voluntary guidance into planning/development agreements over time.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Incentivize lower-carbon materials

Enhance and integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs, like the expedited permitting process and Austin Energy's Green Building program, to transition voluntary design guidance into planning and development agreements over time.
extraction_rationale: Clear incentive/program modification commitment.
mechanism_description: Incentives and development agreements drive adoption of low-carbon materials and deconstruction/reuse practices, lowering embodied greenhouse gas emissions from construction and demolition.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Create a performance framework and educational programming on low-carbon materials best practices for industry professionals and the public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Educate stakeholders on materials best practices

Create a performance framework and educational programming for industry professionals and the general public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color, to reduce the lifecycle and negative health impacts of building materials and construction practices.
extraction_rationale: Specific education/programming intervention tied to embodied carbon and health impacts.
mechanism_description: Providing performance guidance and educational programming increases adoption of low-carbon construction materials and practices, reducing lifecycle (embodied) greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact building materials (e.g., glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, carpet).
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Stimulate decarbonization with local producers installation

Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact materials, specifically glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, and carpet.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention focusing on decarbonizing named material categories via partnerships.
mechanism_description: Partnering with materials suppliers to shift to lower-carbon building products reduces embodied emissions from construction and renovation activities.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Water
policy_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified water-demand target with timeframe and implementation basis; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: Implementing water efficiency and demand-management strategies reduces community water consumption.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Enhance community engagement and partnerships to advance Water Forward equity strategies (e.g., My ATX Water, incentives, ordinances) and implement targeted outreach to improve participation.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Engage residents in water efficiency technological transitions, and conservation programs

Enhance community engagement strategies and create partnerships with community organizations to advance equity through Water Forward strategies, including My ATX Water (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), incentives, and ordinances.
extraction_rationale: Specific engagement and partnership actions to implement water conservation strategies with equity focus.
mechanism_description: Community engagement and partnerships increase participation in water efficiency and conservation programs, which reduces per-capita water demand.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Evaluate water conservation and customer assistance program criteria to remove structural barriers and expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and other low-income-serving programs.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Evaluate water conservation, customer assistance, and workforce development program participation criteria

Evaluate program criteria to identify opportunities to address structural barriers that prevent program participation... explore strategies to expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and increase participation in other programs serving low-income customers.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program evaluation and expansion commitment for equity in water assistance and conservation.
mechanism_description: Removing enrollment barriers expands participation in water conservation programs, which reduces per-capita water consumption and the energy/emissions associated with delivering and treating water.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Assess and pursue water conservation and energy management optimization at the water-energy nexus, including evaluating Austin Water facility energy use and developing methods to quantify emissions reductions from water-demand reduction.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Reduce emissions at the water-energy nexus

Assess how water demand reduction is associated with energy consumption in residential and commercial buildings and Austin Water facilities. Identify and pursue synergistic water conservation and energy management optimization efforts through programs and partnerships.
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment and optimization actions linking water conservation to emissions reductions.
mechanism_description: Implementing water conservation and water-energy optimization reduces water demand, which lowers energy needed for water treatment/pumping and end uses, thereby reducing associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE
policy_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
extraction_rationale: Quantified land-use development siting target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.
mechanism_description: Directing new non-residential growth to activity centers and corridors supports compact, transit-served development patterns that lower car dependence and reduce transportation-related emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Adopt Complete Communities plans to reverse historical and racist impacts, address displacement, and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Plan for complete communities

Adopt Complete Communities plans that reverse historical and racist impacts on areas experiencing displacement and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
extraction_rationale: Specific planning instrument commitment (adopt plans) tied to equity and land use.
mechanism_description: Adopting complete communities plans guides equitable neighborhood preservation and development decisions, strengthening institutional capacity to reduce displacement and improve community resilience.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Work with medium and large employers to locate places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Work with employers on location and amenities

Work with medium and large employers to locate their places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
extraction_rationale: Explicit intervention to influence employer location decisions toward target areas.
mechanism_description: By concentrating workplaces in growth centers and corridors, the policy supports transit-oriented development that reduces vehicle miles traveled and associated transportation emissions while limiting sprawl-related land use impacts.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops with first/last-mile options.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3 : Create mobility hubs

Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops to offer a complete trip experience. These hubs should offer various first- and last-mile mobility options.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/program concept to improve multimodal access.
mechanism_description: Developing mobility hubs with park-and-ride and first/last-mile connections makes transit more convenient, encouraging mode shift from private cars to transit and lowering transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Phase out free employee parking at City facilities and other large employers within 1/2 mile of the transit priority network, replacing it with cash-out, carpool/vanpool, transit passes, telework, or other commuter benefits.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Phase out free parking

Phase out the practice of providing free parking spaces to employees at City of Austin facilities and other large employers located within 1/2 mile of the Austin Strategic Mobility Plan's transit priority network. Instead, offer a parking cash-out, carpool and vanpool options, flex schedules, transit passes, support for teleworking, or other commuter benefits programs.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy change with defined scope and alternative measures.
mechanism_description: Eliminating free parking and offering commuter benefits shifts commuting from single-occupancy driving to transit/carpool/telework, reducing vehicle travel and transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE
policy_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable housing units*, with 75% of new housing located within ½ mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
extraction_rationale: Quantified housing production/preservation target with timeframe and location share; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: Concentrating new housing development near activity centers and corridors enables shorter trips and greater transit/walking use, reducing vehicle travel and associated emissions while supporting more compact land use.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
policy_statement: Engage communities vulnerable to displacement for immediate affordable housing assistance and proactively monitor at-risk affordable properties to extend affordability.
parent_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Offer immediate affordable housing assistance

Engage directly with communities that are vulnerable to displacement and connect them with services. Proactively monitor affordable housing properties at risk of losing their affordability status to extend the period of affordability.
extraction_rationale: Direct programmatic commitment for anti-displacement and affordability preservation.
mechanism_description: Engaging vulnerable residents and monitoring at-risk affordable housing helps preserve affordability and prevent displacement, strengthening community stability and adaptive capacity to climate impacts.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE
policy_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 50% of trips in Austin are made using public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided altogether by working from home*.
extraction_rationale: Quantified mode-shift target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.
mechanism_description: Increase the share of trips made by low-carbon modes or avoided altogether to reduce vehicle travel and cut transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Expand and improve public transportation

Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation commitment to deliver transit improvements through Project Connect.
mechanism_description: Expand and improve public transit service to shift travel from private vehicles to lower-emission modes, reducing transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options and expand free transit options to increase access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Promote free transportation options

Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options.

How we'll get there:

- Expand free transit options, including through Capital Metro, to provide increased transportation access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
extraction_rationale: Concrete access policy (resources + expanding free transit).
mechanism_description: By expanding and publicizing free public transit options, more trips can shift from private vehicles to transit, lowering per-trip emissions and reducing overall transport GHG emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Engage impacted communities to improve transit stops/stations and enforce ADA and accessibility guidelines around transit and public spaces.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Enhance transit stations and stops

Partner with Capital Metro, developers, and community organizers to engage low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities to improve transit stops, stations, and access to these facilities.

How we'll get there:

- Enforce the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Public Rights-of-Way Accessibility Guidelines regulations...
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation and enforcement commitments for equitable, accessible transit facilities.
mechanism_description: Improve and enforce accessibility at transit stops/stations to increase transit usability and ridership, shifting trips from cars to transit and lowering transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Implement 2020 Proposition B bonds for transportation infrastructure (sidewalks, bikeways, trails, safety, safe routes to school, substandard streets) in low-income communities and communities of color through meaningful engagement.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Prioritize bicycle networks

Implement the 2020 Proposition B voter-approved general obligation bonds for transportation infrastructure... in low-income communities and communities of color through a meaningful community engagement process.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure investment implementation tied to a named funding mechanism (Proposition B).
mechanism_description: Investing in sidewalks, bikeways, trails, and safe-routes infrastructure enables more walking and cycling in place of driving, reducing transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Provide access to free or reduced-price bicycles and bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve safety and comfort for riders of color.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Enhance bicycle education and training

Provide access to free or reduced-priced bicycles and basic bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve the comfort and safety of people of color riding bicycles.
extraction_rationale: Specific program/training interventions with equity and safety focus.
mechanism_description: Expanding equitable bicycle access, training, and safer enforcement increases cycling uptake and shifts trips away from cars, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement, and build priority segments while addressing ADA barriers and gaps.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 6: Improve sidewalks, urban trails, and crossings

Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan, emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement.

How we'll get there:

- Build all high- and very-high-priority sidewalk and trail segments and address ADA barriers and gaps...
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning update and build-out commitments with accessibility requirements.
mechanism_description: Expand and improve sidewalks and urban trails to enable more walking and biking trips, reducing vehicle travel and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies, including a City telework policy encouraging at least 85% of eligible City employees to participate and supporting a goal of 15% residents working from home by 2039.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 7: Promote remote and flexible work policies

Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies to maintain and encourage further reductions in single-occupancy vehicle trips.

How we'll get there:

- ...all new City of Austin telework and remote work policies contribute towards the goal of achieving 15% Austin residents working from home by 2039. This should include a strategy to implement a City of Austin telework policy encouraging no less than 85% of eligible City of Austin employees to participate in telework.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy approach with quantifiable participation targets/timeframe.
mechanism_description: Promoting telework and flexible work arrangements reduces commute driving (especially single-occupancy trips), which lowers transportation fuel use and emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION
policy_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text: By 2030, 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin are electrified, and electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse. This translates to approximately 460,000 electric vehicles on the road.
extraction_rationale: Quantified electrification target with timeframe and scale estimate; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: Increase electric vehicle adoption and usage to replace internal-combustion vehicle travel, thereby reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment to inform an EV adoption growth plan, using community partners and hiring residents from the communities served.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Conduct an EV Community Needs Assessment

Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment... The assessment will inform an EV adoption growth plan...
extraction_rationale: Specific planning/assessment commitment tied to equity and EV adoption.
mechanism_description: Conducting an EV community needs assessment improves targeting and design of EV adoption plans, enabling higher EV uptake and associated transport emissions reductions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing EVs, prioritizing low-income communities and communities of color and areas with limited transit access.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Create equitable incentives for buying and leasing EVs

Collaborate with community partners to create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing electric vehicles. This strategy would... prioritize low-income communities and communities of color...
extraction_rationale: Specific incentive program commitment with equity prioritization.
mechanism_description: Provide targeted financial/accessible incentives for EV purchase or leasing to accelerate EV adoption, displacing gasoline/diesel driving and reducing transport greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Partner with mobility authorities to reduce or eliminate toll rates for low-income communities and communities of color who drive EVs on toll roads from the Eastern Crescent to central Austin.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Reduce tolls for EVs in the Eastern Crescent

Partner with highway and regional mobility authorities to offer a reduced or eliminated toll rate for low-income communities and communities of color who drive EVs on toll roads from the Eastern Crescent to central Austin.
extraction_rationale: Concrete pricing policy intervention targeted by geography and equity groups.
mechanism_description: Reducing toll costs for EV drivers incentivizes EV adoption and use, shifting travel away from gasoline vehicles and lowering transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Create an electric bike and car-sharing program centered on low-income communities and communities of color, with income thresholds and actions to address displacement.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Launch an e-bike and electric car-sharing program

Create an electric bike and car-sharing program centered on low-income communities and communities of color to support functional, low-cost, zero-emissions mobility.
extraction_rationale: Specific shared mobility program commitment with equity design features.
mechanism_description: Launching shared e-bikes and electric car-sharing increases access to low/zero-emission travel options, shifting trips away from gasoline vehicles and lowering transport-related emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Encourage public sector fleets in the five-county MSA to commit to buying 100% electric vehicles when available, cost-competitive, and operationally suitable, leveraging purchasing collaboration and considering early retirement of older vehicles.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Electrify public sector fleet vehicles

Encourage vehicle fleets from public sector agencies... to commit to buying 100% electric vehicles when they are available, cost-competitive, and meet operational needs.
extraction_rationale: Fleet procurement commitment framework with conditions and collaboration mechanism.
mechanism_description: Public agencies shift fleet procurement from internal combustion vehicles to electric vehicles, lowering fuel combustion and reducing transportation-sector emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION
policy_statement: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
verbatim_text: By 2030, Austin has a compelling and equitably distributed mix of level 1, 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city... could mean more than 37,000 charging ports.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound infrastructure scale target; followed by detailed strategies.
mechanism_description: Expand and equitably site EV charging stations to remove refueling barriers and enable increased electric vehicle adoption and use.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Equitably distributed charging by 2030
policy_statement: Incentivize installation of EV charging infrastructure by multiple actors and prioritize systematically excluded areas, including installing chargers on publicly owned land to fill gaps.
parent_statement: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Create a network with more low-cost, accessible charging stations

Continue to incentivize the installation of EV charging infrastructure by the City, businesses, auto manufacturers, and third-party charging companies...

- Prioritize areas that have been systematically excluded... 
- Fill in gaps by installing EV charging on publicly owned land in systematically excluded areas...
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure incentive and siting commitments with equity prioritization.
mechanism_description: Incentivizing and installing EV chargers increases charging access, which enables greater EV adoption and supports subsequent transportation emissions reductions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Equitably distributed charging by 2030
policy_statement: Incentivize internet-connected smart charging to support grid reliability, efficiency, reduced emissions, accessibility, and lower costs by 2030.
parent_statement: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Incentivize internet-connected smart charging

By 2030, the City will have a network of intelligent charging... Incentivize internet-connected charging infrastructure with the ability to manage the start and end time and charge rate across 24 hours...
extraction_rationale: Specific technology/incentive commitment with deadline (by 2030).
mechanism_description: Incentivizing internet-connected smart EV chargers enables managed charging and load shifting, improving grid efficiency and renewable integration and reducing emissions from electricity supply used for vehicle charging.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Equitably distributed charging by 2030
policy_statement: Adopt new energy and building codes to address future EV charging needs and lower barriers, including potentially requiring chargers at commercial and multifamily properties and EV-ready single-family homes.
parent_statement: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Adopt new energy and building codes

Adopt new energy and building codes that address future EV charging needs...

How we'll get there:

- Consider... requiring EV chargers at commercial and multifamily properties, and requiring single-family homes to be EV charger-ready.
extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory/code-based intervention for EV readiness and charger requirements.
mechanism_description: Update building/energy codes to require EV chargers or EV-ready wiring, expanding charging availability and thereby enabling greater EV adoption and lower transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Equitably distributed charging by 2030
policy_statement: Expand culturally competent outreach to systematically excluded groups and stakeholders about EV charging incentives and installation.
parent_statement: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Expand outreach to systematically excluded groups

Expand outreach to community groups, professional organizations, unions, and property managers using culturally competent information about EV charging incentives and installation.
extraction_rationale: Specific outreach/communications strategy to increase equitable uptake.
mechanism_description: Culturally competent outreach increases awareness and utilization of EV charging incentives and installation pathways, leading to more charging deployment and enabling higher EV adoption.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION
policy_statement: Ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary/health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, ensure all Austinites can access a food system that is community-driven, addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary and health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound system goal with specific features; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: Support regenerative agricultural practices to increase soil carbon sequestration and improve ecosystem health, reducing net food-system emissions.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION — Goal: Equitable food system access
policy_statement: Apply a purchasing framework and build a regional food system network to increase institutional and corporate procurement of pro-climate, pro-health food options.
parent_statement: Ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary/health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Support institutional food purchasing

Apply a purchasing framework, support supply-chain infrastructure, and build a regional food system network to bolster institutional and corporate food procurement of proclimate, pro-health options.
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional procurement and network-building intervention.
mechanism_description: Implementing a procurement framework and regional food-system network shifts large buyers toward lower-carbon food choices, reducing upstream agricultural and supply-chain greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION — Goal: Equitable food system access
policy_statement: Implement community-driven programs to incentivize and promote affordable, culturally relevant pro-climate, pro-health food choices in prepared and retail options, focusing on minimizing displacement.
parent_statement: Ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary/health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Promote and fund community-driven food retail programs

Implement community-driven programs to incentivize and promote more affordable and culturally relevant pro-climate, pro-health choices in prepared and retail food options, focusing on minimizing displacement.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program commitment targeting retail/prepared food with equity and displacement lens.
mechanism_description: Community-driven incentives and promotion shift prepared and retail food purchases toward lower-carbon, healthier options, reducing lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions from the food system.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION — Goal: Equitable food system access
policy_statement: Develop community-driven programs/tools (e.g., labeling, apps, education, incentives) to equitably make pro-climate, pro-health food choices affordable and culturally relevant.
parent_statement: Ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary/health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Incentivize pro-climate, prohealth food choices

Develop a variety of community-driven programs and tools to equitably engage and empower... to make affordable and culturally relevant pro-climate, prohealth food choices.
extraction_rationale: Specific commitment to develop tools and incentives to change food choices equitably.
mechanism_description: Community-designed labeling, education, apps, and incentives shift diets toward lower-carbon foods, reducing emissions associated with food production and consumption.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION — Goal: Equitable food system access
policy_statement: Conduct a food waste root cause analysis and implement changes to increase food waste reduction practices by 50%, covering single-family, multifamily, and commercial properties.
parent_statement: Ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary/health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Conduct a food waste root cause analysis

Conduct a food waste root cause analysis and implement changes informed by the analysis to increase food waste reduction practices by 50%. These practices should... include single-family, multi-family, and commercial properties.
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment plus quantified reduction action (50%).
mechanism_description: Identifying and addressing root causes of food waste reduces the amount of organic material landfilled, avoiding methane emissions from landfill decomposition.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION
policy_statement: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50% by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions-reduction target in purchasing with deadline; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: Implementing low-carbon purchasing requirements shifts demand toward lower-emissions goods and services, reducing lifecycle/supply-chain greenhouse gas emissions associated with procurement.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION — Goal: 50% purchasing emissions reduction
policy_statement: Develop a methodology to measure lifecycle GHG emissions and other impacts from non-residential purchasing and establish a baseline.
parent_statement: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Measure institutional lifecycle emissions

Develop a methodology to measure lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions and other environmental and social impacts from non-residential purchasing and identify a baseline for tracking progress.
extraction_rationale: Specific measurement/baseline-building commitment supporting the quantified target.
mechanism_description: Establishing lifecycle emissions measurement and a baseline for non-residential purchasing provides data and transparency that can guide and track procurement decisions to reduce supply-chain GHG emissions.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION — Goal: 50% purchasing emissions reduction
policy_statement: Strengthen the City of Austin's Sustainable Procurement Program and adopt/develop sustainability guidelines for high-impact products; educate vendors and staff and integrate procurement with sustainability initiatives.
parent_statement: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Strengthen the City's sustainable purchasing program

Strengthen the City of Austin's Sustainable Procurement Program to serve as a model for others locally and nationally.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to strengthen a named City program (sustainable procurement).
mechanism_description: Implementing sustainable procurement guidelines and education shifts city purchasing toward lower-emission goods and services, reducing embodied and upstream supply-chain greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: FOOD AND PRODUCT CONSUMPTION — Goal: 50% purchasing emissions reduction
policy_statement: Recruit at least 50% of large local institutional purchasers (2,500+ employees) and at least 2,500 local organizations to adopt environmental and social sustainability procurement standards/guidelines, prioritizing historically underutilized and BIPOC-led/employing organizations.
parent_statement: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Strengthen non-City institutional purchasing programs

Recruit at least 50% of local large institutional purchasers (2,500+ employees) and at least 2,500 local organizations of all sizes to collaboratively adopt a set of environmental and social sustainability procurement standards and/or guidelines.
extraction_rationale: Quantified recruitment targets and specified procurement standards adoption action.
mechanism_description: Adopting and spreading sustainability procurement standards changes what organizations buy, shifting demand toward lower-carbon products and services and reducing upstream supply-chain emissions.

section_header: 9.3.3 Living environment and public hygiene
policy_statement: Dakar will pursue adaptation objectives including strengthening climate/coastal/urban pollution data networks; reducing vulnerable populations through awareness; reducing heatwave vulnerability; increasing protection initiatives; reducing air pollutants by 2030; increasing urban green spaces by 150 ha by 2030; expanding ecological urban agriculture by 2030; and increasing climate-resilient infrastructure.
verbatim_text:
Adaptation measures aim to strengthen the resilience of natural and human systems to climate risks and impacts. The objectives set are as follows:
- Strengthen networks for observing, measuring and collecting climate, coastal and urban pollution data.
- Reduce the number of people vulnerable to  the  effects  of  climate  change  through awareness-raising and communication.
-  Reduce the vulnerability of populations to heatwaves.
- Increase initiatives to protect populations against risks and disasters linked to extreme events and climate change.
- Reduce air pollutants by 2030 to improve air quality and lower the rate of respiratory disease.
- Increase urban green spaces by 150 ha in Dakar by 2030
- Increase areas of ecological urban agriculture  and  access  to  better  quality agricultural  products  by  2030  to  strengthen citizens' food security
- Increase the proportion of infrastructure adapted and resilient to climate risks.
extraction_rationale: List of explicit objectives, including quantified targets and deadlines (2030).
mechanism_description: Expand and strengthen climate/coastal/pollution monitoring and data networks to improve information for planning and implementing climate adaptation and resilience measures.

verbatim_text: Environmental Monitoring Information System of Kuwait (eMISK). eMISK is an electronic system established by the Environment Public Authority (EPA) of Kuwait and aims at building and maintaining a comprehensive geo-environmental database of Kuwait and a GIS system to facilitate updating and analyzing environmental data. policy_statement: Build additional desalination capacity: Kuwait produced around 3.85 MCM/d of desalinated water in 2016; eight additional plants were being commissioned and additional IWPP/plant projects are planned, including Al-Zour North (250 MGD), Al-Zour South (145 MGD), Al Khiran (125 MGD), and Doha (60 MGD), with further projects planned to be tendered as of 2019 (~285 MGD additional).

Al-Zour North IWPP (capacity: 250 Million of Gallons per Day (MGD)...), Al-Zour South Plant (capacity: 145 MGD), Al Khiran IWPP (capacity: 125 MGD), and Doha desalination plant (60 MGD)... While, 4 more projects (Al-Nwaiseeb, New Shuaiba, New Doha, Doha RO phase 2) with an additional capacity of around 285 MGD are planned to be tendered as of 2019.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas

| Main objectives: • Reducing biomass fuel • Reducing the speed at which fires spread • Increasing the forest's resilience to fire • Ensuring the safety of people and property
